protocol.h(3) Library Functions Manual protocol.h(3)

NAME

protocol.h - ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int protocol_start_connection ()
Start protocol connection handling. void protocol_stop_connection ()
Stop protocol connection handling. bool protocol_connection_lost ()
Check if connection has been lost. const acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()
Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

Defines the protocol handling interface for client-side packet processing and data reception thread management.

The protocol handler manages the data reception thread, which continuously receives packets from the server, decrypts them, and dispatches them to appropriate handlers based on packet type. It also detects connection loss and signals the main thread to initiate reconnection.

Command Types Handled

The client processes these command/packet types from the server:

  • PACKET_TYPE_ASCII_FRAME: Terminal display frame (rendered to stdout)
  • PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_BATCH: Opus-encoded audio samples (sent to audio playback)
  • PACKET_TYPE_PONG: Keepalive response (recorded for timeout detection)
  • PACKET_TYPE_CLEAR_CONSOLE: Terminal clear command (resets display)
  • PACKET_TYPE_SERVER_STATE: Server state updates (logged for debugging)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

See also

topic_client_protocol "Protocol Architecture"

Definition in file src/client/protocol.h.

Function Documentation

bool protocol_connection_lost ()

Check if connection has been lost. Atomically checks whether the data reception thread has detected connection loss. Connection loss is detected by:

  • Socket read error (recv() returns error)
  • Socket closed by server (recv() returns 0)
  • Decryption failure (corrupted stream)
  • Invalid packet magic number (out of sync)

Once loss is detected, this flag remains true until the next successful connection.

Returns

true if connection lost (detected by protocol thread), false otherwise

Note

This is checked by the main thread to trigger reconnection logic. The flag is set atomically by the protocol thread and checked atomically by main.

See also

server_connection_lost "Signal connection loss (called by protocol thread)"

Check if connection has been lost

Returns

true if protocol detected connection loss, false otherwise

Definition at line 1410 of file client/protocol.c.

1410                                 {
1411   return atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited) || server_connection_is_lost();
1412 }




References atomic_load_bool(), and
    server_connection_is_lost().






const
  acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()


Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch. Returns the packet
    dispatch callbacks structure used to route received packets to appropriate
    handlers. This is primarily used for ACDS signaling packet dispatch and
    WebRTC peer negotiation.




The callbacks structure includes handlers for:


    
  
  • ASCII frame rendering
    • 
  
  • Audio batch processing
    • 
  
  • Server state updates
    • 
  
  • Connection control commands (clear, reset, etc.)
    • 



Returns


Pointer to client callbacks structure (never NULL)


Used by WebRTC sessions to receive and dispatch ACDS signaling
    packets.




Returns pointer to the global callback structure for use by WebRTC
    sessions that need to receive ACDS signaling packets.




Returns


Pointer to client callbacks (never NULL)


Definition at line 957 of file
  client/protocol.c.


957                                                              {
958   return &g_acip_client_callbacks;
959 }










int
  protocol_start_connection ()


Start protocol connection handling. Spawns the data reception
    thread in the global client worker thread pool. The thread begins receiving
    and processing packets from the server. Must be called after the server
    connection is established and the cryptographic handshake is complete.




The thread will:



  
1.

  
Continuously receive packets from the server socket

  
2.

  
Decrypt packets using the negotiated session key

  
3.

  
Dispatch packets to appropriate handlers based on type

  
4.

  
Detect connection loss and signal the main thread




Returns


0 on success, negative on error (thread pool full or
  other resources exhausted)


Note


Must be paired with protocol_stop_connection() to
  properly shut down the thread before reconnection or exit.


See also


protocol_stop_connection "Stop data reception
  thread"



protocol_connection_lost "Check if connection was
    lost"




Start protocol connection handling




Initializes protocol state and starts the data reception thread.
    Must be called after successful server connection establishment.




Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1155 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1155                                 {
1156   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 🟢 PROTOCOL_START: Starting client protocol initialization");
1157 
1158   // Register protocol atomics with named debug registry
1159   static bool protocol_atomics_registered = false;
1160   if (!protocol_atomics_registered) {
1161     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_data_thread_exited, "protocol_data_reception_thread_exit_confirmation", NULL);
1162     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_frames_rendered, "protocol_frames_successfully_rendered", NULL);
1163     protocol_atomics_registered = true;
1164   }
1165 
1166   // Reset protocol state for new connection
1167   g_server_state_initialized = false;
1168   g_last_active_count = 0;
1169   g_should_clear_before_next_frame = false;
1170 
1171   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ STATE_RESET: server_initialized=false, active_count=0, clear_flag=false");
1172 
1173   // Reset display state for new connection
1174   display_reset_for_new_connection();
1175 
1176   // Check if we're on a WebSocket transport (crypto handshake is async via ACIP callbacks)
1177   // For WS/WSS, we must start data_reception FIRST so it can handle the crypto handshake,
1178   // then wait for crypto to complete before sending CAPABILITIES/STREAM_START.
1179   // For TCP, crypto is already done synchronously before this function is called.
1180   acip_transport_t *init_transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1181   bool is_websocket = init_transport &&
1182                       acip_transport_get_type(init_transport) == ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET;
1183 
1184   if (is_websocket) {
1185     // WebSocket: start data reception thread FIRST to handle async crypto handshake.
1186     // TCP does crypto synchronously in connection_attempt.c, but WS crypto flows
1187     // asynchronously via ACIP callbacks in the data_reception thread.
1188     log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Starting data_reception for async crypto handshake");
1189     atomic_store_bool(&g_data_thread_exited, false);
1190     if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, data_reception_thread_func, NULL, 1, "data_reception") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1191       log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to start data reception thread");
1192       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Data reception thread creation failed");
1193       return -1;
1194     }
1195     log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Data thread spawned, waiting for crypto handshake");
1196 
1197     // Wait for crypto handshake to complete (CRYPTO_PARAMETERS → KEY_EXCHANGE → HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE)
1198     // Skip waiting if encryption is disabled (no_encrypt mode)
1199     if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
1200       const int MAX_CRYPTO_WAIT_MS = 10000; // 10 seconds
1201       const int POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 10;
1202       int waited_ms = 0;
1203       while (!crypto_client_is_ready() && !should_exit() && server_connection_is_active() &&
1204              waited_ms < MAX_CRYPTO_WAIT_MS) {
1205         platform_sleep_us(POLL_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
1206         waited_ms += POLL_INTERVAL_MS;
1207         if (waited_ms % 1000 == 0) {
1208           log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS_CRYPTO: Waiting for handshake... (%d ms elapsed)", waited_ms);
1209         }
1210       }
1211 
1212       if (!crypto_client_is_ready()) {
1213         if (should_exit() || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1214           log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Connection lost during crypto handshake");
1215         } else {
1216           log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Crypto handshake timed out after %d ms", MAX_CRYPTO_WAIT_MS);
1217         }
1218         return -1;
1219       }
1220       log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Crypto handshake completed after %d ms", waited_ms);
1221     } else {
1222       log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Encryption disabled, skipping handshake wait");
1223     }
1224   }
1225 
1226   // Send CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet (after crypto is ready for both TCP and WS)
1227   // Server expects this as the first packet after crypto handshake
1228   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Sending CLIENT_CAPABILITIES (terminal size)");
1229   asciichat_error_t cap_result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
1230                                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
1231   if (cap_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1232     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to send terminal size capabilities");
1233     return -1;
1234   }
1235 
1236   // Send STREAM_START packet with combined stream types
1237   uint32_t stream_types = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO; // Always have video
1238   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
1239     stream_types |= STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO; // Add audio if enabled
1240   }
1241   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Sending STREAM_START: types=0x%x (video%s)", stream_types,
1242             (stream_types & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) ? "+audio" : "");
1243   asciichat_error_t stream_result = threaded_send_stream_start_packet(stream_types);
1244   if (stream_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1245     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to send stream start");
1246     return -1;
1247   }
1248 
1249   // Start data reception thread (TCP only - WS already started it above for crypto)
1250   if (!is_websocket) {
1251     log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] TCP: Starting data reception thread");
1252     atomic_store_bool(&g_data_thread_exited, false);
1253     if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, data_reception_thread_func, NULL, 1, "data_reception") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1254       log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to start data reception thread");
1255       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Data reception thread creation failed");
1256       return -1;
1257     }
1258   }
1259 
1260   // Start webcam capture thread
1261   log_debug("Starting webcam capture thread...");
1262   if (capture_start_thread() != 0) {
1263     log_error("Failed to start webcam capture thread");
1264     return -1;
1265   }
1266   log_debug("Webcam capture thread started successfully");
1267 
1268   // Initialize audio sender thread BEFORE starting audio capture
1269   // This ensures sender is ready when capture thread starts queueing packets
1270   // Must happen after connection succeeds to prevent deadlock if connection fails
1271   audio_sender_init();
1272 
1273   // Start audio capture thread if audio is enabled
1274   log_debug("Starting audio capture thread...");
1275   if (audio_start_thread() != 0) {
1276     log_error("Failed to start audio capture thread");
1277     return -1;
1278   }
1279   log_debug("Audio capture thread started successfully (or skipped if audio disabled)");
1280 
1281   // Start keepalive/ping thread to prevent server timeout
1282   log_debug("Starting keepalive/ping thread...");
1283   if (keepalive_start_thread() != 0) {
1284     log_error("Failed to start keepalive/ping thread");
1285     return -1;
1286   }
1287   log_debug("Keepalive/ping thread started successfully");
1288 
1289   g_data_thread_created = true;
1290   return 0;
1291 }






References ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), audio_sender_init(),
    audio_start_thread(), capture_start_thread(),
    crypto_client_is_ready(), display_reset_for_new_connection(),
    g_client_worker_pool, GET_OPTION,
    keepalive_start_thread(), log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, platform_sleep_us(),
    server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), should_exit(),
    STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO, STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO,
    terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(), thread_pool_spawn(),
    threaded_send_stream_start_packet(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






void
  protocol_stop_connection ()


Stop protocol connection handling. Signals the data reception
    thread to exit and waits for it to join. Performs proper cleanup to prevent
    resource leaks and race conditions during reconnection or shutdown.




The function:



  
1.

  
Closes the server socket (interrupts blocking recv() in data thread)

  
2.

  
Sets the global shutdown flag (signals thread to exit)

  
3.

  
Waits for the data reception thread to join (polls for thread exit)




This must be called before reconnecting to prevent socket
    descriptor leaks and to ensure the thread has fully cleaned up before
  reuse.




Note


This function blocks until the data reception thread
  exits. Typical wait time: <100ms (socket closure immediately interrupts
  recv()).


See also


protocol_start_connection "Start data
  reception thread"


Stop protocol connection handling




Gracefully shuts down the data reception thread and cleans up
    protocol state. Safe to call multiple times.


Definition at line 1301 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1301                                 {
1302   // Log final frame count before shutting down (always log for diagnostic purposes)
1303   int final_frame_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_frames_rendered);
1304   log_info("📊 CLIENT SESSION STATS: %d unique frames rendered during connection", final_frame_count);
1305 
1306   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 1. Starting protocol_stop_connection");
1307 
1308   // In snapshot mode, data reception thread was never started, but capture thread may still be running
1309   // Always stop the capture thread to prevent use-after-free when transport is destroyed
1310   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
1311     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: flushing H.265 encoder before stopping capture thread");
1312     // Flush the encoder to output any buffered frames before exiting
1313     if (g_h265_encoder) {
1314       // Allocate buffer large enough for worst-case H.265 encoded frame
1315       // Using 320x240 (typical test resolution) * 2x multiplier + header
1316       size_t flush_buf_size = 5 + (320 * 240 * 2);
1317       uint8_t *flush_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(flush_buf_size, uint8_t *);
1318       size_t flush_size = flush_buf_size;
1319       asciichat_error_t flush_result = h265_encoder_flush(g_h265_encoder, flush_buf, &flush_size);
1320       if (flush_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && flush_size > 0) {
1321         log_info("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Flushed H.265 encoder: got %zu bytes of buffered frames", flush_size);
1322         // Send the flushed frame if we got one
1323         acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1324         if (transport && server_connection_is_active()) {
1325           uint32_t width = 320, height = 240; // TODO: track actual dimensions
1326           log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Sending flushed frame: %ux%u, %zu bytes", width, height, flush_size);
1327         }
1328       }
1329       SAFE_FREE(flush_buf);
1330     }
1331     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: stopping capture thread before returning");
1332     capture_stop_thread();
1333     return;
1334   }
1335 
1336   // Don't call signal_exit() here - that's for global shutdown only!
1337   // We just want to stop threads for this connection, not exit the entire client
1338 
1339   // Shutdown the socket FIRST to interrupt any blocking network operations in worker threads
1340   // This must happen BEFORE audio_stop_thread() so audio sender thread unblocks from network send
1341   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 2. About to call server_connection_shutdown");
1342   server_connection_shutdown();
1343   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 3. server_connection_shutdown() returned");
1344 
1345   // Signal audio sender thread to exit
1346   // Must happen after socket shutdown so any blocked network calls fail
1347   // Audio sender is created in ALL modes except snapshot mode
1348   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 4. About to call audio_stop_thread");
1349   audio_stop_thread();
1350   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 5. audio_stop_thread() returned");
1351 
1352   // Early return if data thread was never created (e.g., mirror mode)
1353   // In mirror mode, we only need to stop the audio sender (done above)
1354   if (!g_data_thread_created) {
1355     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 6. Data thread not created, returning");
1356     return;
1357   }
1358 
1359   // Stop keepalive/ping thread - it checks connection status and will exit
1360   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 7. About to call keepalive_stop_thread");
1361   keepalive_stop_thread();
1362   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 8. keepalive_stop_thread() returned");
1363 
1364   // Stop webcam capture thread
1365   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 9. About to call capture_stop_thread");
1366   capture_stop_thread();
1367   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 10. capture_stop_thread() returned");
1368 
1369   // Wait for data reception thread to exit gracefully
1370   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 11. Waiting for data thread to exit");
1371   // Thread checks should_exit() every read cycle (typically <1-5ms), so timeout can be much shorter
1372   int wait_count = 0;
1373   while (wait_count < 5 && !atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1374     platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms * 5 = 50ms max wait
1375     wait_count++;
1376   }
1377 
1378   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1379     log_warn("Data thread not responding after 50ms - will be joined by thread pool");
1380   }
1381   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 12. Data thread wait complete");
1382 
1383   // Join all threads in the client worker pool (in stop_id order)
1384   // This handles the data reception thread and (eventually) all other worker threads
1385   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 13. About to call thread_pool_stop_all");
1386   if (g_client_worker_pool) {
1387     asciichat_error_t result = thread_pool_stop_all(g_client_worker_pool);
1388     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1389       log_error("Failed to stop client worker threads");
1390       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Thread pool stop failed");
1391     }
1392   }
1393   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 14. thread_pool_stop_all() returned");
1394 
1395   g_data_thread_created = false;
1396 
1397 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1398   log_debug("Data reception thread stopped and joined by thread pool");
1399 #endif
1400   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 15. protocol_stop_connection complete");
1401 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_load_u64(), audio_stop_thread(),
    capture_stop_thread(), g_client_worker_pool,
    g_h265_encoder, GET_OPTION, h265_encoder_flush(),
    keepalive_stop_thread(), log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    platform_sleep_us(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), server_connection_shutdown(),
    thread_pool_stop_all(), and US_PER_MS_INT.
